3.happening: Place the left pointer at the first character.
3.why: It marks one end of the pair we'll swap.
3.invariant: Everything left of `left` is already reversed.

4.happening: Place the right pointer at the last character.
4.why: It marks the other end of the pair.
4.invariant: Everything right of `right` is already reversed.

5.happening: While the pointers haven't met, a pair still needs swapping.
5.why: Once they meet or cross, every character is in its final place.
5.invariant: left < right means unreversed characters remain in the middle.

6.happening: Swap the two ends: s[{left}] ({s[left]}) and s[{right}] ({s[right]}).
6.why: Exchanging the outermost unreversed pair puts both characters in their final spots.
6.invariant: After the swap, indices {left} and {right} hold their final characters.

7.why: The outer pair is done, so move toward the middle.
7.invariant: left has advanced past a finished character.

8.why: Same for the right side — close in on the middle.
8.invariant: right has retreated past a finished character.

9.happening: The pointers met — the string is fully reversed. Return it.
9.why: Each character was swapped exactly once, using no extra array (O(1) space).

init.happening: Placing a pointer at each end.
init.why: We'll swap inward from both ends.
init.invariant: left and right bound the unreversed middle.

loop.happening: Checking whether the pointers have met.
loop.why: The work is done once they cross.
loop.invariant: Characters outside [left, right] are already reversed.

update.why: One swap fixes both ends of the remaining range.
update.invariant: The outermost unreversed pair becomes final.

move.happening: Stepping both pointers toward the middle.
move.invariant: The unreversed range shrinks by two.

return.why: Done in one pass with O(1) extra memory.
